title: Sano Sauro
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Sano Sauro |homeworld=[[Eeropha]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]] |masters= |apprentices= }} '''Sano Sauro''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] prosecutor for the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] and later the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of [[Eeropha]] for a number of decades near the end of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He had a number of political confrontations with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], particularly [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|There's got to be something we can do! We can't let him win. He's probably laughing at us right now!"
"You should not be concerned with Senator Sauro's reaction. What does it matter if a corrupt man laughs at us? It should be less than the whisper of a gnatfly's wings to us."
"He has made fools of us."
"No, Padawan. If your path is the right one, no one has that power. Those who seek to make fools of others are fools themselves.|16-year-old [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and his master, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]|''[[Jedi Quest: The School of Fear]]''}} ===Prosecutor=== In [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], Sauro served as the prosecutor for [[Vox Chun]], the father of Jedi hopeful, [[Bruck Chun]]. Bruck, who had aided the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Xanatos]] in infiltrating the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], was accidentally killed by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] in the Jedi Temple. He [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's Senate hearing|interrogated]] Obi-Wan, who was accused of killing Bruck intentionally, and fellow Jedi [[Bant Eerin/Legends|Bant Eerin]], who was almost drowned intentionally by Bruck. Sauro tried to find them guilty, exaggerating and twisting the truth. However, the Jedi were proven innocent despite his attempts.''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: Deceptions]]'' ===Senator & benefactor=== Over the next few decades, Sauro became [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] of [[Eeropha]]. During this time, he discovered a young [[Granta Omega]] and sponsored him so that he could attend the [[All Science Research Academy]] on [[Yerphonia]].''[[Jedi Quest: The Master of Disguise]]''''[[Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap]]'' A few years before the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Obi-Wan Kenobi discovered that Senator Sauro had ties to Omega; ties that Sauro denied and made sure that Kenobi could not prove. He, in fact, was the head of a committee that in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]] sponsored a [[Mission to Haariden|scientific expedition]] to [[Haariden]], which was part of a secret plot with Omega to obtain [[titanite]]. ===Supreme Chancery aspirant=== In [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], Sauro helped his friend Omega again (as well as himself), this time politically. He and newly elected Senator [[Bog Divinian]] began to make [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jedi]] accusations in order to dissolve the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate's]] support of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. A vote was to be held in the Senate over whether or not the Jedi should be involved in Senate activities. This was all part of Omega and Sauro's master scheme. By leaking [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]'s drug called the [[Zone of Self-Containment]] into the [[Senate utility tunnels|ventilation shafts]] of the Senate, as well as assassinating [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], Sauro's supporters could easily garner him a seat as the new Supreme Chancellor of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. This would also give Omega unlimited power in destroying the Jedi. However, thanks to the actions of Obi-Wan Kenobi, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]], and [[Ferus Olin]], this plot was [[Attack on the Senate Building|thwarted]]. When the actual Senate vote came, Sauro turned his back on Bog Divinian, and sided with those who were against banning funding to the Jedi Order. His reasons for doing this were because he was offered the position of [[Deputy Chancellor]] by Palpatine himself, which quickly accepted high office, however, tempted Sauro with even more power. Palpatine's true motives for creating the new position were, of course, based on political expediency, in order to transform a rabid political enemy into a quick 'friend'—but more to the point, to maintain a close eye on Sauro's actions, by having now reined him in.''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]'' ===Deputy Chancellor & office emblems=== In his office, Senator Sano Sauro kept a broken [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] sold to him by a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] and which was rumored to have belonged to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]. Sauro prized the [[Claing bush|claing-bush]] thorns as emblems not only of his planet Eeropha, but also of his protégé, [[Granta Omega]], a native of the planet's [[Nierport VII|moon]]. Four of the tall red thorns rose from the corners of Sauro's long but low Senate office desk, "stabbing the air." And, upon [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] appointment of Sauro to the position of [[Deputy Chancellor]] (done to keep Sauro on a leash), in a temporary garden in the exterior courtyard outside the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] complex, twin horns of the bloodred bush could be seen towering above the exotic flowers there. ===Imperial strategist & prosecutor=== Under the emerging [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], Sauro was already ingratiated into Palpatine's inner circle. Sauro hand-picked [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial advisors]] and sent them to strategic planets in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. By [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], Sauro owned a ship called the ''[[True Justice]]'' which held speedy trials to quickly put people accused of political crimes in prison. Two of these prisoners were [[Roan Lands]] and [[Dona Telamark]]. During their trial, however, the two were rescued by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Fy-Tor-Ana|Solace]], the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Oryon]], and [[Trever Flume]], all friends of Ferus Olin.''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]'' ===Naval Academy schoolmaster=== Palpatine was aware of Sauro's plans to use [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] children to train pilots in hope of creating an invincible fleet. He allowed him to keep his plan—for the time being. But when Sano tried to prevent Ferus' friends from escaping, the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] was asking to see him. Sauro soon found himself in a job as the headmaster of the [[Imperial Naval Academy (Coruscant)|Imperial Naval Academy]], which he considered a demotion from his previous position as Senator.''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' He was ordered by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader to find a [[Cadet/Legends|cadet]] with undivided loyalty to the Empire on a need-to-know basis project. He decided to send [[Lune Oddo Divinian]], the Force-sensitive son of his former protege Bog Divinian. He did this to take revenge on Bog, who had risen above him in the Imperial hierarchy by becoming governor of the [[Leemurtoo system]].''[[The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: Deceptions]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The Master of Disguise]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The School of Fear]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Return of the Dark Side]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' {{Hologram}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Master of Deception]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Reckoning]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Sano Sauro |es=Sano Sauro }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sauro, Sano}} [[Category:Agents of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Galactic Empire senators]] [[Category:Galactic Republic bureaucrats]] [[Category:Galactic Republic senators]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Legal professionals]] [[Category:Males]]
